task: I’m cleaning up a bunch of old project files and trying to set up a better system before the next round starts. Can you help me sketch a simple structure for folders, labels, and backups that won’t turn into a headache later?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a simple, low-friction system that should stay usable even when projects pile up.

## 1) Use one standard folder structure
Keep every project in the same shape so you don’t have to think about where things live.

**Example:**
- `01_Admin`
- `02_Reference`
- `03_Working`
- `04_Exports`
- `05_Deliverables`
- `06_Archive`

If you want it even simpler:
- `Admin`
- `Refs`
- `Working`
- `Exports`
- `Deliverables`
- `Archive`

## 2) Separate working files from final files
This is the big one.

- **Working** = active project files, messy drafts, in-progress edits
- **Exports** = temporary renders, review files, test outputs
- **Deliverables** = only the final approved versions
- **Archive** = old versions, completed handoff packages, dead ends

That keeps you from accidentally handing off the wrong file.

## 3) Use a naming convention that tells you version and status
A simple pattern:

**`ProjectName_Date_Version_Status`**

Example:
- `ClientA_2026-04-29_v01_working`
- `ClientA_2026-04-29_v02_review`
- `ClientA_2026-04-29_v03_final`

A few rules:
- keep names short but specific
- use underscores instead of spaces
- never overwrite a final file
- increment versions clearly: `v01`, `v02`, `v03`

If you want a cleaner ending:
- `draft`
- `review`
- `approved`
- `final`

## 4) Add labels or tags only where they help
If your system supports tags, use a small set:
- `active`
- `review`
- `approved`
- `final`
- `archive`
- `needs_qc`

Don’t over-label. Too many tags becomes its own mess.

## 5) Backups: keep 3 copies if possible
A practical backup setup:

1. **Working copy** on your main drive  
2. **Local backup** on an external drive or second disk  
3. **Offsite/cloud backup** for recovery if hardware fails  

That way you’re not relying on one machine.

## 6) Back up at the right moments
At minimum:
- before major edits
- before renaming or reorganizing files
- before export/handoff
- at the end of each work session

If a project is messy, make a **locked backup first** before you touch anything.

## 7) Keep a simple tracking note
Even a plain text file or spreadsheet helps.

Columns:
- current name
- intended name
- location
- status
- notes
- client-approved? yes/no

This is especially useful when old files don’t line up cleanly.

## 8) Make one folder the “source of truth”
Decide where the real active version lives and stick to it.  
Everything else should be either:
- a backup
- a review copy
- a final deliverable
- an archive

That avoids the “which file is the real one?” problem.

If you want, I can turn this into a **ready-to-use folder template** with example names for your kind of projects.

user_simulator.text:
Pretty close, but it’s still a little generic. I’d want it geared more toward post work — like where the camera originals live, where audio gets parked, project files vs transcodes, and a clean way to separate client reviews from actual finals.

Also, I’d rather have something I can drop into a real workflow, not just a concept. Like an actual template with example names for a doc or commercial job, plus a backup routine that won’t get annoying when I’m already buried.

If you can tighten it around that, that’d be useful.

turns.agent_response:
Yep — here’s a more post-specific version you can actually use without it turning into a filing project of its own.

## Simple post folder structure

Use one top-level folder per job:

**`CLIENT_PROJECT_YYYYMMDD`**

Inside it:

- `01_ADMIN`
- `02_CAMERA_ORIGINALS`
- `03_AUDIO_ORIGINALS`
- `04_PROJECT_FILES`
- `05_TRANSCODES`
- `06_REVIEW_EXPORTS`
- `07_FINALS`
- `08_ARCHIVE`
- `09_BACKUPS_NOTES`

### What goes where

**01_ADMIN**
- brief
- call sheets
- release forms
- emails / notes PDFs
- edit logs

**02_CAMERA_ORIGINALS**
- untouched camera media
- card dumps
- checksum reports
- keep this read-only if possible

**03_AUDIO_ORIGINALS**
- field recorder files
- scratch audio
- music licenses if relevant
- any production sound paperwork

**04_PROJECT_FILES**
- NLE project files
- autosaves
- project database files
- graphics project files if needed

**05_TRANSCODES**
- proxies
- optimized media
- temp transcodes
- anything made for editing, not delivery

**06_REVIEW_EXPORTS**
- client review cuts
- watermarked exports
- burn-in versions
- temp review links/files

**07_FINALS**
- approved master exports
- delivery encodes
- textless / M&E / alternate versions if needed

**08_ARCHIVE**
- old versions
- superseded exports
- wrapped project snapshots
- anything you don’t want in active circulation

**09_BACKUPS_NOTES**
- tracking sheet
- version log
- QC notes
- backup checklist
- handoff notes

---

## Clean naming convention

Keep it boring and consistent:

**`Client_Project_ShotOrVersion_Date_Status`**

Examples:
- `Acme_SpringSpot_v01_2026-04-29_review`
- `Acme_SpringSpot_v03_2026-04-29_client`
- `Acme_ProductDoc_v05_2026-04-29_final`

For camera/audio/transcodes, use source-style names:

- `A_CAM_A001_C001_2026-04-29`
- `AUDIO_REC_001_2026-04-29`
- `PROXY_A001_1080p`
- `REV_03_BURNIN`
- `FINAL_MASTER_1080p`
- `FINAL_SOCIAL_9x16`

### Rule of thumb
- **Originals stay untouched**
- **Project files get versioned**
- **Review exports are clearly marked**
- **Finals are final, not “final_final2”**

---

## Example: commercial job

**Top folder:**  
`Acme_SpringSpot_2026-04-29`

**Inside:**
- `01_ADMIN`
- `02_CAMERA_ORIGINALS`
  - `A_CAM`
  - `B_CAM`
  - `CHECKSUMS`
- `03_AUDIO_ORIGINALS`
  - `MIXER`
  - `SFX`
  - `MUSIC`
- `04_PROJECT_FILES`
  - `Premiere`
  - `AE`
  - `AutoSaves`
- `05_TRANSCODES`
  - `Proxies`
  - `Optimized`
- `06_REVIEW_EXPORTS`
  - `Acme_SpringSpot_v01_review.mp4`
  - `Acme_SpringSpot_v02_client_notes.mp4`
- `07_FINALS`
  - `Acme_SpringSpot_v03_master.mov`
  - `Acme_SpringSpot_v03_h264.mp4`
  - `Acme_SpringSpot_v03_social_9x16.mp4`
- `08_ARCHIVE`
- `09_BACKUPS_NOTES`

---

## Example: doc job

**Top folder:**  
`Client_DocTitle_2026-04-29`

**Inside:**
- `01_ADMIN`
- `02_CAMERA_ORIGINALS`
  - `INTERVIEWS`
  - `BROLL`
  - `STILLS`
- `03_AUDIO_ORIGINALS`
  - `INTERVIEW_AUDIO`
  - `ROOM_TONE`
  - `MUSIC`
- `04_PROJECT_FILES`
- `05_TRANSCODES`
  - `INTERVIEW_PROXIES`
  - `BROLL_PROXIES`
- `06_REVIEW_EXPORTS`
  - `DocTitle_v01_review.mp4`
  - `DocTitle_v02_client_notes.mp4`
- `07_FINALS`
  - `DocTitle_v05_master.mov`
  - `DocTitle_v05_web.mp4`
- `08_ARCHIVE`
- `09_BACKUPS_NOTES`

---

## Backup routine that won’t get annoying

Keep it simple enough that you’ll actually do it.

### Daily
- save project files normally
- let autosaves run
- at the end of the day, copy the active project folder to a backup location

### Before risky changes
Always make a quick snapshot before:
- relinking media
- renaming folders
- major version jumps
- conforming
- handoff/export

### At milestones
Make a frozen backup when you hit:
- sync lock
- picture lock
- client approval
- final delivery

### Best low-friction setup
Use **two backup layers**:

1. **Local backup**
   - external drive or second internal drive
   - fast, easy, daily

2. **Offsite backup**
   - cloud or remote copy
   - slower, but protects against theft/failure

If you’re buried, the minimum useful habit is:

- keep originals untouched
- duplicate the project folder before major changes
- never overwrite finals
- archive old review exports instead of deleting them

---

## A practical versioning rule

Use this:

- `v01` = first assembled cut
- `v02` = internal revision
- `v03` = client review
- `v04+` = changes after notes
- `final` only when approved

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-page copy/paste template** you can drop into a job handoff doc or Notion page.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, this is closer. Still want it a bit more real-world and less tidy-on-paper.

Two things I’d tighten:
- I’d separate **camera originals / audio originals / project media / transcodes / reviews / finals** more explicitly, so there’s no ambiguity when you’re moving fast.
- I’d want the backup routine to match actual post habits — like what gets copied after ingest, what gets frozen at picture lock, and what can just live in the working area until the job is done.

If you can, give me a version that reads like something I could actually use on a doc or commercial tomorrow — folder tree, naming examples, and a backup rhythm that doesn’t turn into extra admin every day.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a more **real post workflow** version you could drop into a doc or commercial job tomorrow.

## 1) Top-level job folder

Use one job folder per project:

**`CLIENT_PROJECT_YYYY-MM-DD`**

Example:
- `Acme_SpringSpot_2026-04-29`
- `Northline_DocSeriesEp03_2026-04-29`

Inside that, keep these buckets:

- `00_ADMIN`
- `01_INGEST`
- `02_CAMERA_ORIGINALS`
- `03_AUDIO_ORIGINALS`
- `04_PROJECT_MEDIA`
- `05_PROJECT_FILES`
- `06_REVIEW`
- `07_FINALS`
- `08_ARCHIVE`
- `09_BACKUPS_LOG`

---

## 2) What lives where

### `00_ADMIN`
Planning and paperwork only:
- brief
- schedule
- notes
- releases
- cue sheets
- change notes
- client emails saved as PDF if needed

### `01_INGEST`
This is the working intake area right after dump:
- card copies
- checksum reports
- ingest logs
- sync reports
- any “not yet sorted” material

Once checked, material gets moved out of here.

### `02_CAMERA_ORIGINALS`
**Untouched source media.**
- camera card dumps
- original folder structure preserved if possible
- read-only once verified

Examples:
- `A_CAM_CARD_001`
- `B_CAM_CARD_002`
- `FX6_CARD_014`

### `03_AUDIO_ORIGINALS`
**Untouched sound files.**
- recorder WAVs
- lavs
- boom mixes
- scratch audio
- production sound reports

Examples:
- `MIXER_001`
- `MIXER_002`
- `SFX_WILDTRACKS`
- `MUSIC_LICENSES` if relevant

### `04_PROJECT_MEDIA`
All the stuff created for editing, not delivery:
- proxies
- transcodes
- optimized media
- generated waveforms/cache if you keep them with the job
- synced selects if that’s your workflow

Examples:
- `PROXIES`
- `TRANSCODES`
- `SYNCED_MEDIA`
- `GFX_TEMP`

### `05_PROJECT_FILES`
Actual edit project files:
- NLE project
- autosaves
- bins
- databases
- AE/Resolve/Premiere project files if used

Examples:
- `Premiere_Project_v01.prproj`
- `Premiere_Project_v02.prproj`
- `Resolve_Project.db`
- `AutoSaves/`

### `06_REVIEW`
Only things the client is allowed to comment on:
- review exports
- watermarked versions
- burn-ins
- low-res links
- temp audio mixes for approval

Examples:
- `Acme_SpringSpot_v01_review.mp4`
- `Acme_SpringSpot_v02_client_notes.mp4`
- `Acme_SpringSpot_v03_burnin.mp4`

### `07_FINALS`
Only approved deliverables:
- master
- web
- social
- textless
- M&E
- alternate aspect ratios
- captions if delivered as files

Examples:
- `Acme_SpringSpot_v05_MASTER.mov`
- `Acme_SpringSpot_v05_WEB.mp4`
- `Acme_SpringSpot_v05_9x16.mp4`

### `08_ARCHIVE`
Anything superseded or no longer active:
- old exports
- old project snapshots
- replaced graphics
- retired transcodes
- wrapped job copies

### `09_BACKUPS_LOG`
Not media — just the paper trail:
- backup log
- restore test notes
- QC checklist
- handoff log
- version tracker

---

## 3) Naming rules that won’t fight you later

Keep it simple:

**`Client_Project_Version_Date_Status`**

Examples:
- `Acme_SpringSpot_v01_2026-04-29_review.mp4`
- `Acme_SpringSpot_v03_2026-04-29_client_notes.mp4`
- `Acme_SpringSpot_v05_2026-04-29_final.mov`

For source media, don’t rename the actual camera/audio files unless your workflow requires it. If you do need working names, keep them traceable:

- `A001_C001_001`
- `A001_C002_001`
- `MIXER_001.wav`

For review/final exports, be explicit:
- `review`
- `client_notes`
- `approved`
- `final`

Avoid:
- `final_final`
- `latest`
- `newest`
- `good_one`

---

## 4) What gets copied when

This is the part that keeps it practical.

### After ingest
Copy and verify:
- camera originals
- audio originals
- checksum or verification report
- ingest log

Then:
- move originals into `02_CAMERA_ORIGINALS` and `03_AUDIO_ORIGINALS`
- create proxies/transcodes into `04_PROJECT_MEDIA`
- start project files in `05_PROJECT_FILES`

### During the edit
Daily life should mostly stay in:
- `05_PROJECT_FILES`
- `04_PROJECT_MEDIA`
- `06_REVIEW`

You do **not** need to keep recopying the entire camera roll every day unless the job is changing structure or you’re rebuilding the media set.

### At picture lock
Freeze a snapshot of:
- project files
- current review export
- current QC notes
- any final linked graphics or mix refs

That snapshot becomes your fallback if notes or conform issues come up.

### At final delivery
Copy out:
- approved finals
- final project file snapshot
- deliverable list
- QC log
- handoff notes

Then archive the job as a complete package.

---

## 5) Backup rhythm that stays sane

Use a **three-tier habit**:

### A. Ingest backup
Right after media comes in:
1. copy card/media to working storage
2. verify checksum or copy verification
3. copy the same ingest to backup storage
4. log it

This is the “don’t lose the only copy” step.

### B. Working backup
During the job:
- project files autosave normally
- once per day, copy `05_PROJECT_FILES` and any changed `04_PROJECT_MEDIA` items to backup storage
- don’t keep re-backing up unchanged camera originals every time unless you’re rebuilding from scratch

### C. Milestone snapshot
Freeze a backup at:
- sync complete
- rough cut
- picture lock
- client approval
- final delivery

That’s the version you’ll care about if something breaks.

---

## 6) Low-friction backup rule

If you’re buried, use this rule:

### Back up:
- project files
- new transcodes
- new graphics
- review exports
- notes/logs

### Don’t keep re-copying every day:
- untouched camera originals
- untouched audio originals
- old archive material

Those should already live in a verified ingest location and a backup location.

---

## 7) Example commercial job

**Top folder:**  
`Acme_SpringSpot_2026-04-29`

**Inside:**
- `00_ADMIN`
- `01_INGEST`
- `02_CAMERA_ORIGINALS`
  - `A_CAM_CARD_001`
  - `B_CAM_CARD_002`
- `03_AUDIO_ORIGINALS`
  - `MIXER_001`
  - `MIXER_002`
- `04_PROJECT_MEDIA`
  - `PROXIES`
  - `TRANSCODES`
- `05_PROJECT_FILES`
  - `Acme_SpringSpot_v01.prproj`
  - `AutoSaves`
- `06_REVIEW`
  - `Acme_SpringSpot_v01_review.mp4`
  - `Acme_SpringSpot_v02_client_notes.mp4`
- `07_FINALS`
  - `Acme_SpringSpot_v05_MASTER.mov`
  - `Acme_SpringSpot_v05_WEB.mp4`
  - `Acme_SpringSpot_v05_9x16.mp4`
- `08_ARCHIVE`
- `09_BACKUPS_LOG`

---

## 8) Example doc job

**Top folder:**  
`Northline_DocEp03_2026-04-29`

**Inside:**
- `00_ADMIN`
- `01_INGEST`
- `02_CAMERA_ORIGINALS`
  - `INTERVIEWS`
  - `BROLL`
- `03_AUDIO_ORIGINALS`
  - `INTERVIEW_RECORDER`
  - `ROOM_TONE`
- `04_PROJECT_MEDIA`
  - `PROXIES`
  - `SYNCED`
- `05_PROJECT_FILES`
- `06_REVIEW`
  - `Northline_DocEp03_v01_review.mp4`
  - `Northline_DocEp03_v02_notes.mp4`
- `07_FINALS`
  - `Northline_DocEp03_v06_MASTER.mov`
  - `Northline_DocEp03_v06_WEB.mp4`
- `08_ARCHIVE`
- `09_BACKUPS_LOG`

---

## 9) The simplest version of the routine

If you want the shortest usable version:

- ingest to `01_INGEST`
- verify
- move originals to `02_CAMERA_ORIGINALS` and `03_AUDIO_ORIGINALS`
- build proxies/transcodes in `04_PROJECT_MEDIA`
- keep edits in `05_PROJECT_FILES`
- send client review only from `06_REVIEW`
- put approved deliverables only in `07_FINALS`
- archive old versions in `08_ARCHIVE`
- log backups in `09_BACKUPS_LOG`

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-page checklist** for ingest day / edit day / picture lock / delivery so it’s even easier to use under pressure.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s the shape. Still a little too clean though.

What I’d want next is the actual **day-to-day rhythm** in plain terms — not just where stuff lives, but what I touch first when media comes in, what gets duplicated right away, and what I stop worrying about until lock.

Also worth tightening one thing: in real jobs, **project media** and **transcodes/proxies** can get messy fast if they’re not split hard. Same with review exports vs finals. I’d want the template to make those separations idiot-proof, because that’s usually where people accidentally burn themselves.

So yeah — if you can do a tighter pass with:
- ingest flow
- edit-day backup habit
- picture lock snapshot
- final delivery package

...that’d be the useful version.

turns.agent_response:
Yep — here’s the more operational version, with the splits made harder so it stays usable when the day gets messy.

## Core rule
Keep these as separate buckets, no exceptions:

- **Camera originals** = untouched source media
- **Audio originals** = untouched sound recordings
- **Project media** = synced media, generated media, cache-adjacent working media
- **Transcodes / proxies** = edit-friendly copies only
- **Review exports** = anything the client can comment on
- **Finals** = approved deliverables only

If a file could confuse someone later, it goes in the more specific bucket.

---

# 1) Folder structure

**Top job folder**
`CLIENT_PROJECT_YYYY-MM-DD`

Inside:

- `00_ADMIN`
- `01_INGEST`
- `02_CAMERA_ORIGINALS`
- `03_AUDIO_ORIGINALS`
- `04_PROJECT_MEDIA`
- `05_TRANSCODES_PROXIES`
- `06_PROJECT_FILES`
- `07_REVIEW_EXPORTS`
- `08_FINALS`
- `09_ARCHIVE`
- `10_BACKUP_LOGS`

## What goes where

### `01_INGEST`
Only for the intake phase.
- card dumps
- checksum reports
- ingest notes
- sync reports
- any “not yet sorted” media

### `02_CAMERA_ORIGINALS`
Untouched camera media only.
- keep card structure if possible
- don’t edit here
- don’t mix in proxies

### `03_AUDIO_ORIGINALS`
Untouched audio only.
- recorder WAVs
- scratch tracks
- production sound reports
- music refs if needed, but not working mix files

### `04_PROJECT_MEDIA`
Working media created for the edit that is not a proxy/transcode.
- synced clips if your workflow uses them
- generated media tied to the project
- temp graphics
- any media that belongs to the edit but isn’t source or proxy

### `05_TRANSCODES_PROXIES`
Only edit-friendly copies.
- proxies
- transcodes
- optimized media

Keep this separate from `04_PROJECT_MEDIA` so nobody mistakes it for source or working media.

### `06_PROJECT_FILES`
Actual edit files.
- NLE project
- autosaves
- databases
- bin files
- sequence backups if you save them separately

### `07_REVIEW_EXPORTS`
Client-facing review only.
- burn-ins
- watermarked exports
- temp review cuts
- note rounds

### `08_FINALS`
Only approved delivery files.
- master
- web
- social
- textless
- M&E
- captions if delivered as files

### `09_ARCHIVE`
Old or superseded material.
- old versions
- replaced exports
- retired graphics
- wrapped job copies

### `10_BACKUP_LOGS`
Not media.
- backup log
- QC notes
- handoff notes
- version tracker

---

# 2) Naming convention

Use one simple rule:

**`Client_Project_v##_YYYY-MM-DD_status`**

Examples:
- `Acme_SpringSpot_v01_2026-04-29_review`
- `Acme_SpringSpot_v03_2026-04-29_notes`
- `Acme_SpringSpot_v05_2026-04-29_final`

For source media, keep original naming if possible.

If you need working names, keep them traceable:
- `A001_C001`
- `A001_C002`
- `MIXER_001.wav`

For review exports:
- `review`
- `notes`
- `burnin`

For finals:
- `master`
- `web`
- `9x16`
- `textless`

Avoid:
- `latest`
- `final_final`
- `new`
- `goodone`

---

# 3) Day-to-day rhythm

## When media comes in
Do this first:

1. **Copy card/media to `01_INGEST`**
2. **Verify the copy**
3. **Make a backup copy immediately**
4. **Move verified originals to `02_CAMERA_ORIGINALS` and `03_AUDIO_ORIGINALS`**
5. **Build proxies/transcodes into `05_TRANSCODES_PROXIES`**
6. **Start project files in `06_PROJECT_FILES`**

### What you do not do yet
- don’t clean up old versions
- don’t reorganize finals
- don’t worry about archive structure
- don’t over-label everything

The goal is: ingest cleanly, verify once, then move on.

---

## On edit days
Most of the day should live in:

- `06_PROJECT_FILES`
- `04_PROJECT_MEDIA`
- `05_TRANSCODES_PROXIES`
- `07_REVIEW_EXPORTS`

### Practical habit
- save project files normally
- let autosaves run
- only touch originals if you need to relink or verify
- don’t keep duplicating camera originals every day

### What you back up daily
- project files
- autosaves
- new review exports
- new graphics
- new transcodes/proxies
- notes/logs

### What usually stays put
- untouched camera originals
- untouched audio originals
- old archive material

Those should already be safely copied and verified.

---

# 4) Backup rhythm that matches actual post work

## After ingest
This is the important one.

- copy media to working storage
- verify the copy
- make a second copy to backup storage
- log it

That covers the “don’t lose the only copy” moment.

## During the edit
Once per day:
- back up `06_PROJECT_FILES`
- back up anything new in `04_PROJECT_MEDIA`
- back up any new `05_TRANSCODES_PROXIES`
- back up new review exports if they matter

## Before risky moves
Always snapshot before:
- relinking
- renaming folders
- changing drive structure
- rebuilding proxies
- conforming
- major version jumps

## At picture lock
Freeze a clean snapshot of:
- project files
- current review export
- relevant media links
- QC notes
- change log

That becomes your safe fallback.

## At final delivery
Back up the delivery package:
- approved finals
- final project file
- QC log
- version list
- handoff notes

Then archive the job as a complete set.

---

# 5) Picture lock snapshot

At lock, make one folder or package that contains:

- locked project file
- latest approved review export
- QC notes
- change log
- final graphics refs
- final mix refs if relevant

Example:
`Acme_SpringSpot_PICTURE_LOCK_2026-05-03`

Inside:
- `ProjectFiles`
- `Review`
- `QC`
- `Notes`
- `Refs`

This is the version you want if anything has to be rebuilt later.

---

# 6) Final delivery package

When it’s done, keep the delivery package clean:

`Acme_SpringSpot_FINAL_DELIVERY_2026-05-06`

Inside:
- `08_FINALS`
  - `Acme_SpringSpot_v05_master.mov`
  - `Acme_SpringSpot_v05_web.mp4`
  - `Acme_SpringSpot_v05_9x16.mp4`
- `QC`
- `Handoff_Notes`
- `Version_Log`
- `Project_Snapshot`

That way the final handoff is separate from the working mess.

---

# 7) The simplest usable rule set

If you want the short version:

- **Ingest first**
- **Verify immediately**
- **Separate originals from working media**
- **Keep proxies/transcodes in their own folder**
- **Keep review exports separate from finals**
- **Back up project files daily**
- **Freeze a snapshot at picture lock**
- **Package finals separately at delivery**

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-page doc template** with:
- folder tree
- naming examples
- ingest checklist
- edit-day backup checklist
- picture-lock checklist
- delivery checklist

That’d be the most usable version.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s the blunt, usable version.

---

# 1) Ingest checklist

## Do this first
- [ ] Create job folder: `CLIENT_PROJECT_YYYY-MM-DD`
- [ ] Confirm drive space on working + backup storage
- [ ] Create these folders if they don’t exist:
  - `01_INGEST`
  - `02_CAMERA_ORIGINALS`
  - `03_AUDIO_ORIGINALS`
  - `04_PROJECT_MEDIA`
  - `05_TRANSCODES_PROXIES`
  - `06_PROJECT_FILES`
  - `07_REVIEW_EXPORTS`
  - `08_FINALS`
  - `09_ARCHIVE`
  - `10_BACKUP_LOGS`

## Copy and verify
- [ ] Copy camera cards into `01_INGEST`
- [ ] Copy audio cards into `01_INGEST`
- [ ] Run checksum / verification
- [ ] Confirm copy matches source
- [ ] Make a second backup copy to backup storage
- [ ] Log what was copied, date, and any issues in `10_BACKUP_LOGS`

## Sort after verification
- [ ] Move camera originals to `02_CAMERA_ORIGINALS`
- [ ] Move audio originals to `03_AUDIO_ORIGINALS`
- [ ] Build proxies/transcodes into `05_TRANSCODES_PROXIES`
- [ ] Put edit-generated media in `04_PROJECT_MEDIA`
- [ ] Start project files in `06_PROJECT_FILES`

## Don’t do yet
- [ ] Don’t rename finals
- [ ] Don’t clean archive
- [ ] Don’t mix proxies with originals
- [ ] Don’t delete anything until verified and backed up

---

# 2) Edit-day backup checklist

## Start of day
- [ ] Open project from `06_PROJECT_FILES`
- [ ] Confirm you’re on the current version
- [ ] Check autosave is active

## During the day
- [ ] Save project normally
- [ ] Let autosaves run
- [ ] Keep review exports in `07_REVIEW_EXPORTS`
- [ ] Keep new working media in `04_PROJECT_MEDIA`
- [ ] Keep proxies/transcodes in `05_TRANSCODES_PROXIES`

## End of day backup
- [ ] Copy `06_PROJECT_FILES` to backup storage
- [ ] Copy any new or changed files in `04_PROJECT_MEDIA`
- [ ] Copy any new or changed files in `05_TRANSCODES_PROXIES`
- [ ] Copy any new review exports from `07_REVIEW_EXPORTS`
- [ ] Copy updated notes/logs from `10_BACKUP_LOGS`
- [ ] Confirm backup completed
- [ ] Record backup date in `10_BACKUP_LOGS`

## Before risky moves
Do a backup first if you are:
- [ ] relinking media
- [ ] renaming folders
- [ ] rebuilding proxies
- [ ] changing drive structure
- [ ] conforming
- [ ] jumping major versions

---

# 3) Picture-lock snapshot checklist

## Before lock
- [ ] Confirm the cut is locked
- [ ] Confirm the right project version is open
- [ ] Confirm latest approved review export is in `07_REVIEW_EXPORTS`
- [ ] Confirm QC notes are complete

## Make the lock snapshot
- [ ] Copy the locked project file from `06_PROJECT_FILES`
- [ ] Copy the approved review export from `07_REVIEW_EXPORTS`
- [ ] Copy QC notes from `10_BACKUP_LOGS`
- [ ] Copy change log / version notes
- [ ] Copy any final graphics refs or mix refs if relevant

## Save it in one folder
Create:
`CLIENT_PROJECT_PICTURE_LOCK_YYYY-MM-DD`

Inside:
- `ProjectFiles`
- `Review`
- `QC`
- `Notes`
- `Refs`

## Final check
- [ ] Confirm snapshot opens
- [ ] Confirm version/date are written clearly
- [ ] Confirm this folder is separate from working files

---

# 4) Final delivery checklist

## Before export
- [ ] Confirm approved version
- [ ] Confirm final picture lock is the source
- [ ] Confirm final audio is correct
- [ ] Confirm titles, captions, and aspect ratios if needed

## Export finals
Put final exports in `08_FINALS`:
- [ ] master
- [ ] web
- [ ] social / alternate aspect ratios
- [ ] textless or M&E if required
- [ ] captions if delivered as files

Example names:
- `Client_Project_v05_master.mov`
- `Client_Project_v05_web.mp4`
- `Client_Project_v05_9x16.mp4`

## Package delivery
Create:
`CLIENT_PROJECT_FINAL_DELIVERY_YYYY-MM-DD`

Inside:
- `08_FINALS`
- `QC`
- `Handoff_Notes`
- `Version_Log`
- `Project_Snapshot`

## Final checks
- [ ] Confirm file names are clean
- [ ] Confirm version numbers are correct
- [ ] Confirm dates are included
- [ ] Confirm no review files are mixed in with finals
- [ ] Confirm the delivery package is separate from working files
- [ ] Confirm backup of the delivery package exists

---

# Folder separation rule
Keep these hard-separated:

- `02_CAMERA_ORIGINALS` = camera source only
- `03_AUDIO_ORIGINALS` = audio source only
- `04_PROJECT_MEDIA` = working media
- `05_TRANSCODES_PROXIES` = proxies/transcodes only
- `07_REVIEW_EXPORTS` = client review only
- `08_FINALS` = approved deliverables only

---

If you want, I can turn this into a **single copy-paste checklist page** with checkboxes and blank lines for notes, so it reads like a real job template.
